title: 5 Proverbs a man of God builds his 20s on
caption:
Work isn't the curse.

Pointless work is. A man drifting through his 20s and calling it "waiting on God" isn't trusting — he's avoiding.

Scripture has a lot to say about the man who actually shows up. Proverbs especially.

About laziness — and how quietly it robs a man of years.
About excellence — and how skill built in private always shows up in public.
About planning — and the difference between working hard and working wise.
About correction — and why the man who deflects hard truth stays exactly where he is.
About brotherhood — because you can't sharpen yourself.

"Do you see someone skilled in their work? They will serve before kings." — Proverbs 22:29

The man building in obscurity isn't being overlooked. He's being prepared.

Your 20s aren't practice. What you build now is the man you walk into the rest of your life as.

—

Comment "build" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.
